title: Functions, Transformations, Inverses, and Composition · Mastery Check
summary: Model sensor calibration and reversible conversions across representations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a calibration and conversion library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define function domain, codomain, range, units, and practical interpretation.
Predict how translations, scaling, reflection, and composition change a model.
Construct and validate inverses under one-to-one domain restrictions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Multi Representation for a calibration and conversion library.
steps.why: The Multi Representation result for a calibration and conversion library is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Multi Representation, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a calibration and conversion library. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
